original_abstract:
In celebration of the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project, we present a high-performance AI/TP (Artificial Intelligence / Theorem Proving) system that significantly advances the state of the art in formal mathematics. Our system successfully proves approximately 60% of the theorems in the Mizar Mathematical Library (MML) in a fully automated "hammer" setting. When provided with the specific premises used in human-written proofs, this performance increases to 75%. 

We provide a comprehensive account of the methodologies and large-scale experiments that enabled these results. Key technical contributions include the integration of the E and Vampire provers with machine-learning modifications—specifically the ENIGMA and Deepire systems—and the deployment of various learning-based premise selection techniques. Central to our success is an incremental "prove-learn" loop that interleaves the generation of millions of automated proofs with the training of increasingly sophisticated neural and gradient-boosted models. Finally, we showcase a selection of complex Mizar problems that can now be solved entirely automatically, reflecting the significant progress made in the field over the last decade.
humanized_abstract:
To mark the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project we are releasing a new high-performance Artificial Intelligence / Theorem Proving system for formal mathematics, capable of bringing the field of formal mathematics to a new era. Experiments show that our system is able to fully automatically prove some 60% of the theorems in the Mizar Mathematical Library (MML) in the “hammer” mode, where all it gets is the statement of a theorem. Increasing this percentage to 75% is possible when the additional premises that a human proofwriter needs to introduce from the knowledge base in order to prove a theorem are provided.

We present in detail the methods and the large-scale experiments that led to the aforementioned results. Some key technical contributions include: 1) the combination of the E prover with some machine learning improvements that we implemented (as part of the ENIGMA and Deepire projects) 2) the premise selection learning-based techniques 3) an incremental “prove-learn” loop for training increasingly complex neural and gradient-boosted models by generating millions of automated proofs. We finish the paper with some illustrations of fully automatically solvable Mizar problems that have been shown to be hard over the last decade.

Some key technical contributions include: 1) the combination of the E prover with some machine learning improvements that we implemented (as part of the ENIGMA and Deepire projects) 2) the premise selection learning-based techniques 3) an incremental “prove-learn” loop for training increasingly complex neural and gradient-boosted models by generating millions of automated proofs. We finish the paper with some illustrations of fully automatically solvable Mizar problems that have been shown to be hard over the last decade.
document.input:
In celebration of the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project, we present a high-performance AI/TP (Artificial Intelligence / Theorem Proving) system that significantly advances the state of the art in formal mathematics. Key technical contributions include the integration of the E and Vampire provers with machine-learning modifications—specifically the ENIGMA and Deepire systems—and the deployment of various learning-based premise selection techniques.
